pois.name: Kloster Maulbronn
pois.description: Founded in 1147, this Cistercian abbey is the most complete and best-preserved medieval monastery complex north of the Alps. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site known for its transition from Romanesque to Gothic architecture.

pois.name: Heidelberger Schloss
pois.description: A world-famous ruin that dominates the skyline of Heidelberg, combining elements of Gothic and Renaissance styles. It served as the residence of the Princes of the Palatinate and is a major landmark of German Romanticism.

pois.name: Freiburger Münster
pois.description: The cathedral of Freiburg im Breisgau features a unique openwork spire that was completed around 1330. It is one of the few large Gothic churches in Germany that was finished during the Middle Ages.

pois.name: Burg Hohenzollern
pois.description: The ancestral seat of the imperial House of Hohenzollern, this 19th-century fortress sits atop Mount Hohenzollern. It is a masterpiece of military architecture and neo-Gothic revival style.

pois.name: Ulmer Münster
pois.description: This Lutheran church holds the record for the tallest church tower in the world, reaching 161.5 meters. Construction began in 1377 and was finally completed in 1890.

pois.name: Klosterinsel Reichenau
pois.description: Located on Lake Constance, this island features three historic churches belonging to a former Benedictine monastery. It is a UNESCO World Heritage site famous for its early medieval wall paintings.

pois.name: Burg Meersburg
pois.description: Often cited as the oldest inhabited castle in Germany, it overlooks Lake Constance. It served as a residence for the Prince-Bishops of Constance and later the poet Annette von Droste-Hülshoff.

pois.name: Schloss Lichtenstein
pois.description: Perched on a cliff edge in the Swabian Jura, this Romantic Gothic Revival castle was inspired by Wilhelm Hauff's novel Lichtenstein. It was built between 1840 and 1842.

pois.name: Schloss Bruchsal
pois.description: The residence of the Prince-Bishops of Speyer is famous for its elaborate Baroque staircase designed by Balthasar Neumann. It is considered one of the finest examples of ecclesiastical architecture in Germany.

pois.name: Schloss Schwetzingen
pois.description: The former summer residence of the Electors Palatine is primarily famous for its vast, intact formal gardens. The complex blends French formal style with English landscape garden elements.

pois.name: Triberger Wasserfälle
pois.description: One of Germany's highest waterfalls, descending 163 meters over seven stages along the Gutach River. It is a signature natural landmark of the Black Forest region.

pois.name: Blautopf
pois.description: A karst spring in Blaubeuren known for its intense blue color caused by light scattering in the deep water. It is the source of the Blau river and the entrance to a vast cave system.

pois.name: Kloster Wiblingen
pois.description: This former Benedictine abbey near Ulm features one of the most magnificent Rococo libraries in the world. The library hall is renowned for its colorful ceiling frescoes and ornate statues.

pois.name: Kaiserpfalz Bad Wimpfen
pois.description: The largest remaining Hohenstaufen imperial palace north of the Alps. The historic skyline of the town is characterized by its Blue Tower and Red Tower.

pois.name: Kloster Schöntal
pois.description: A former Cistercian abbey in the Jagst valley featuring a stunning Baroque abbey church and staircase. It served as a final resting place for several members of the Berlichingen family.

pois.name: Kloster Alpirsbach
pois.description: This 900-year-old Benedictine monastery in the Black Forest features a stark Romanesque church and a late Gothic cloister. It is one of the few monasteries where original medieval monk cells are preserved.

pois.name: Schloss Sigmaringen
pois.description: The residence of the Hohenzollern-Sigmaringen family, this castle stands prominently on a rock over the Danube. It houses one of Europe's largest private weapon collections.

pois.name: Burg Rötteln
pois.description: One of the largest medieval castle ruins in South Baden, situated near Lörrach. It offers panoramic views over the Wiese valley towards Switzerland and France.

pois.name: Mummelsee
pois.description: A high-altitude cirque lake on the Schwarzwaldhochstraße, steeped in local legends about a lake king and water sprites. It is a popular starting point for hikes to the Hornisgrinde.

pois.name: Alte Brücke Heidelberg
pois.description: The Karl-Theodor-Brücke is a monumental stone bridge built in the 18th century crossing the Neckar. It is one of the last major examples of classical stone bridge construction in Germany.

pois.name: Kloster Bebenhausen
pois.description: Tucked away in the Schönbuch forest, this complex includes a Cistercian monastery and a later royal hunting lodge. It is exceptionally well-preserved and serves as a quiet retreat near Tübingen.

pois.name: Kloster Salem
pois.description: Once one of the wealthiest Cistercian abbeys, this complex features a grand Gothic cathedral and a vast Baroque residence. It currently houses an elite boarding school.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Ludwigsburg
pois.description: One of Europe's largest original Baroque palace complexes, often called the 'Versailles of Swabia'. It consists of 18 buildings set in extensive gardens and was the seat of the Kings of Württemberg.

pois.name: Gasthaus zum Roten Bären
pois.description: Located in Freiburg, this is recognized as the oldest inn in Germany. Records of the building date back to the founding of the city in the early 12th century.

pois.name: Badische Staatsbrauerei Rothaus
pois.description: Situated high in the Black Forest, this historic brewery was founded by the Abbey of St. Blasien. It is famous for its 'Tannenzäpfle' beer and traditional brewing methods.

pois.name: Altes Rathaus Esslingen
pois.description: A striking half-timbered building with a Renaissance facade and an astronomical clock. It is a centerpiece of one of the most intact medieval city centers in Germany.

pois.name: Feldberg Gipfel
pois.description: The highest peak in the Black Forest at 1,493 meters. On clear days, the summit offers panoramic views extending from the Vosges in France to the Swiss and Austrian Alps.

pois.name: Festung Hohenasperg
pois.description: A former hill fortress that served as a state prison for centuries, earning the nickname 'Tränenberg' (Mountain of Tears). It provides extensive views over the surrounding Ludwigsburg area.

pois.name: Heuneburg
pois.description: The remains of an early Celtic hillfort located above the Danube, once a major trade hub in the 6th century BCE. It is considered one of the most important archaeological sites of the Iron Age in Central Europe.

pois.name: Isteiner Klotz
pois.description: A prominent limestone cliff rising above the Rhine valley, featuring the small St. Vitus Chapel built into the rock. It was historically a strategic military fortification.

pois.name: Wimsener Höhle
pois.description: The only water cave in Germany accessible by boat. It is associated with local Swabian legends and serves as the primary drainage for the surrounding karst plateau.

pois.name: Burg Hornberg
pois.description: The former castle of Götz von Berlichingen, the famous 'Knight with the Iron Hand'. It is situated high above the Neckar valley and is surrounded by productive vineyards.

pois.name: Kloster Bronnbach
pois.description: A former Cistercian abbey located in the Tauber valley, known for its Romanesque church and late Baroque refectory. The site reflects the influence of the order on agricultural and cultural life in Franconia.

pois.name: Schloss Weikersheim
pois.description: The seat of the Lords of Hohenlohe is renowned for its grand 'Knights' Hall' and its perfectly preserved Baroque garden featuring a gallery of stone dwarves.

pois.name: Dom St. Blasien
pois.description: This abbey church features a massive dome that is 36 meters wide, making it one of the largest in Europe. It was built in neoclassical style after a devastating fire in the 18th century.

pois.name: Wallfahrtskirche Birnau
pois.description: A masterpiece of Vorarlberg Baroque architecture overlooking Lake Constance. The interior is lavishly decorated with Rococo ornaments and features the 'Honey-eater' cherub statue.

pois.name: Kloster Lorch
pois.description: A former Benedictine monastery and the burial site of the Hohenstaufen dynasty. It is situated on a hill overlooking the Rems valley and contains a famous circular painting of the Hohenstaufen history.

pois.name: Burg Wildenstein
pois.description: Perched high above the Danube Gorge, this early modern fortress is one of the best-preserved examples of its kind. It currently serves as one of Germany's most popular youth hostels.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Urach
pois.description: A late Gothic palace that served as the residence for the Dukes of Württemberg during the partition of the territory. It features one of the few preserved Gothic ballrooms in Germany.

pois.name: Hohenkarpfen
pois.description: A distinctive cone-shaped mountain in the Swabian Jura. It was the site of a medieval castle and now offers a 360-degree view of the Baar region and the Black Forest.

pois.name: Schloss Favorite
pois.description: A Baroque 'pleasure palace' built for Margravine Sibylla Augusta. It is the oldest German 'porcelain palace' and remains largely unchanged, preserving its original interiors.

pois.name: Kloster Hirsau
pois.description: Once the site of one of the most influential Benedictine abbeys in Europe, the ruins now host open-air performances. The site played a key role in the 11th-century monastic reform movement.

pois.name: Residenzschloss Rastatt
pois.description: Built by Margrave Ludwig Wilhelm of Baden-Baden, this was the first Baroque residence modeled after Versailles on the Upper Rhine. It houses the Military History Museum.

pois.name: Burg Hohenneuffen
pois.description: The largest ruin on the edge of the Swabian Jura, this fortress was never conquered. It played a significant role in the history of the state during the 1948 Three-State Conference.

pois.name: Federsee
pois.description: A moorland lake surrounded by a vast nature reserve, famous for its archaeological finds of prehistoric pile dwellings. The site is part of the UNESCO World Heritage 'Prehistoric Pile Dwellings around the Alps'.

pois.name: Schloss Solitude
pois.description: A Rococo palace commissioned by Duke Karl Eugen of Württemberg as a hunting lodge and retreat. It is connected to the Ludwigsburg Palace by a straight 13-kilometer-long avenue.

pois.name: Altstadt Schiltach
pois.description: A picturesque town in the Kinzig valley known for its exceptionally well-preserved ensemble of timber-framed houses. It was historically a center for rafting and tanning.

pois.name: Wutachschlucht
pois.description: Often referred to as the 'Grand Canyon of the Black Forest', this river canyon features steep rock walls and diverse flora. It is a prime example of active geological erosion.

pois.name: Erzabtei Beuron
pois.description: The founding monastery of the Beuronese Congregation, situated in the dramatic Upper Danube Valley. It is known for its distinct 19th-century Beuronese Art school that influenced modern church art.

pois.name: Karlsbrücke Besigheim
pois.description: A historic stone bridge crossing the Enz river in one of Germany's most beautiful wine towns. It connects the medieval lower town with the upper fortifications.

pois.name: Barockschloss Mannheim
pois.description: The second-largest Baroque palace complex in Europe after Versailles. It was the residence of the Electors of the Palatinate and now houses the University of Mannheim.

pois.name: Kloster Stift Neuburg
pois.description: A Benedictine abbey located on the banks of the Neckar near Heidelberg. It combines a historic church with modern agricultural operations and a popular monastery brewery.

pois.name: Burgruine Staufeneck
pois.description: A hilltop castle ruin near Salach, once a seat of the Lords of Staufeneck. It offers a commanding view over the Filstal and serves as a local landmark.

pois.name: Stiftskirche Stuttgart
pois.description: The main church of the Evangelical-Lutheran State Church in Württemberg, located in the city center. Its two asymmetrical towers are a signature feature of Stuttgart's skyline.

pois.name: Grabkapelle auf dem Württemberg
pois.description: Built by King Wilhelm I for his wife Queen Catherine, this mausoleum stands on the former site of the ancestral castle of the House of Württemberg. It is a prominent example of neoclassical architecture.

pois.name: Burg Guttenberg
pois.description: An inhabited medieval castle that was never destroyed. It is famous for its German Falconry Center and the historical museum documenting the life of the castle's residents through the centuries.

pois.name: Kloster Comburg
pois.description: A former Benedictine monastery in Schwäbisch Hall that looks more like a fortress with its encircling walls. It houses a unique Romanesque chandelier, the 'Hartwig-Leuchter'.

pois.name: Altes Schloss Stuttgart
pois.description: A 10th-century water castle that was expanded into a Renaissance residence for the Dukes of Württemberg. It now houses the Landesmuseum Württemberg.

pois.name: Ruine Honberg
pois.description: The remains of a fortress in Tuttlingen, known for its two restored towers. It serves as a cultural venue and offers panoramic views of the city and the Danube.

pois.name: Uracher Wasserfall
pois.description: A scenic waterfall in the Swabian Jura where the water drops 37 meters over a tufa limestone ledge. It is one of the most visited natural sites in the Biosphere Reserve of the Swabian Jura.
